title: Link-Bearing Elements, Relations, Base URLs, and Crawl Graph · Language Lens: Edge Evidence
summary: Only anchor tags are inspected, every href becomes a page, relation and base semantics disappear, and fetched URL sets stand in for a graph. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go edge evidence increment in semantic provenance-rich link graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Store source and target identity, element, attribute, raw value, relation, scope decision, and observation separately from fetch state.
Reject or classify fragment, empty, malformed, mailto, tel, javascript, data, credentials, and unsupported schemes before scheduling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Edge Evidence · Theory Edge Evidence

theory case — A theory Go team handles case 4063 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Build Edge Evidence evidence · Theory Edge Evidence
steps.instruction:

Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

checks.description: The cumulative go portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-go-edge-evidence-m3ev76.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-edge-evidence-m3ev76(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_edge_evidence_m3ev76\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?linkCases < 18)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?classifiedLinks < 17 \|\| classifiedLinks > linkCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?relationCases < 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unexplainedEdges > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using store and source plus specific case evidence.
